module_title: Test Module - Introduction to Machine Learning

options: The science of getting computers to learn without explicit programming
questions.explanation: Machine learning is the science of getting computers to learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed.
questions.learning_outcome: Understand the definition of machine learning

questions.question_text: Python is one of the most popular languages for machine learning.
questions.explanation: Python is indeed one of the most popular programming languages for ML due to its rich ecosystem of libraries like NumPy, Pandas, and scikit-learn.
questions.learning_outcome: Recognize common ML tools and technologies

questions.question_text: Name three Python libraries commonly used for machine learning.
questions.correct_answer: NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn (or TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, matplotlib, etc.)
questions.explanation: Popular ML libraries include NumPy (numerical computing), Pandas (data manipulation), scikit-learn (ML algorithms), TensorFlow/PyTorch (deep learning), and matplotlib (visualization).
questions.learning_outcome: Identify common ML tools and libraries

questions.question_text: Which ML application (stock prediction, face detection, sentiment analysis, or chatbots) relates most to your industry or interests? Explain why in 2-3 sentences.
questions.explanation: There is no single correct answer. Reflect on your own context and how ML applications might apply to your work or interests.
questions.learning_outcome: Connect ML concepts to personal context
questions.reviewer_notes: Good reflection question to personalize learning

questions.question_text: You need to train a complex deep learning model on a large dataset, but your laptop only has a CPU. What are three solutions you learned about to address this computational limitation?
questions.correct_answer: 1) Use Google Colab with free GPU access, 2) Enable GPU runtime in Colab settings, 3) Use cloud computing services like AWS/GCP with GPU instances (or similar cloud-based solutions)
questions.explanation: This module introduced cloud-based solutions for GPU access, with Google Colab being the primary free option for beginners. Cloud platforms provide scalable computing without expensive hardware.
questions.learning_outcome: Apply knowledge of cloud computing for ML

questions.question_text: What is the primary advantage of using Google Colab for machine learning?
options: It has the best code editor available
Free GPU access in the cloud without local installation
It is required for all Python programming
It is faster than all local machine setups
questions.explanation: Google Colab's main advantage is providing free GPU access through a cloud-based Jupyter notebook environment, eliminating the need for expensive local GPU hardware.
questions.learning_outcome: Understand benefits of cloud computing for ML

questions.question_text: You need to install Python on your computer before using Google Colab.
questions.explanation: Google Colab runs entirely in the browser with Python pre-installed in the cloud environment. No local installation is needed.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT one of the five demo applications shown in Session 1?
questions.explanation: The five demos were: stock predictor, cricket predictor, face detection, sentiment analyzer, and chatbot. Weather forecasting was not included.

questions.question_text: Explain in one sentence what a Jupyter notebook is and why it's useful for machine learning.
questions.correct_answer: A Jupyter notebook is an interactive coding environment that combines code, visualizations, and explanatory text in one document, making it ideal for ML experimentation and sharing results.
questions.explanation: Jupyter notebooks allow you to write code in cells, see immediate output, create visualizations, and add markdown explanations, all in one place - perfect for the iterative nature of ML development.

questions.question_text: After seeing the five demo applications, what excites you most about learning machine learning? What challenges do you anticipate?
questions.explanation: Reflect honestly on your motivation and concerns. Common excitement areas include automation possibilities and solving real problems. Common challenges include math/statistics background and understanding algorithms.
questions.learning_outcome: Self-reflect on learning goals and challenges
